mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 3.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 3.5% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.88% is near historical easing threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159,626 thousand reflects stable employment base
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) in months with PAYEMS growth > 150k versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reduce equity allocations.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month by > 2% in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 733,258M and HOUST at 1,307k show moderate demand levels
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100.5 signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors flee cyclical exposure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 100.5 and remains below this level for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 101.43 is above threshold but showing potential vulnerability
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals persistent monetary tightening that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 324.37 and FEDFUNDS at 3.88% near trigger threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.3 percentage points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.4% is historically moderate but vulnerable to shocks
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 23,770.98B reflects ongoing economic activity
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals broad-based demand strength and labor market tightness that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% AND UNRATE declines by > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:Current levels show moderate demand and stable labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of co-triggered instances, or the three-way interaction is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 2-year rolling window.
